name: claude-code-cache-fix
description: Auto-starts the claude-code-cache-fix proxy for Claude Code in VS Code. Fixes prompt-cache instability that causes excessive quota burn. Tracks upstream npm v3.1.0.

commands.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Show Status
Claude Code Cache Fix: Enable Proxy Mode
Claude Code Cache Fix: Disable Proxy Mode
Claude Code Cache Fix: Open Proxy Request Log
Claude Code Cache Fix: Test Connection to Anthropic API
Claude Code Cache Fix: Export Windows Corporate Certificates

claude-code-cache-fix.autoStartProxy.markdownDescription:
**When `true` (default):** on activate, the extension silently enables proxy mode — sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` in global `claudeCode.environmentVariables` and spawns the local proxy server as a managed child process. No command-palette step needed.

**When `false`:** nothing auto-starts. Use the command palette (`Claude Code Cache Fix: Enable Proxy Mode`) to turn it on manually.

Proxy mode is the only supported mode in v0.7.0+ — preload-mode wrapper support was dropped because it doesn't work on the Bun-binary CC (v2.1.113+) anyway.

claude-code-cache-fix.autoInstallInterce.markdownDescription:
**When `true` (default):** if the `claude-code-cache-fix` npm package is missing on activate, the extension installs it using whichever package manager owns the Node toolchain — `volta install` when `npm` resolves to a Volta shim, otherwise `npm install -g`. Progress surfaced via a VS Code notification.

**When `false`:** a warning popup appears asking you to run the install yourself.

The extension needs the npm package for the proxy server; without it, nothing works.

claude-code-cache-fix.prefixDiff.markdownDescription:
**Diagnostic.** Activates the v3.1.0 `prefix-diff` proxy extension by setting `CACHE_FIX_PREFIXDIFF=1` in the proxy spawn env. Snapshots request prefixes to `~/.claude/cache-fix-snapshots/` so you can `diff` across turns and find what's busting the cache. Does not mutate requests.

Large output — can fill gigabytes over a long session. Leave off unless actively investigating a cache-instability report.

configuration.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Corporate environments (proxies, custom CAs)

claude-code-cache-fix.httpsProxy.markdownDescription:
**Corporate HTTP(S) proxy URL** for upstream forwarding to `api.anthropic.com`. Required behind Zscaler / Netskope / Forcepoint / Bluecoat / corporate squid.

Example: `http://proxy.corp.example:8080`

Leave empty to use the system `HTTPS_PROXY` / `HTTP_PROXY` environment variables. When set, this is passed into the proxy child's environment; the proxy itself honors it via `hpagent` (requires `claude-code-cache-fix@>=3.0.3`).

**Verify:** run `Claude Code Cache Fix: Test Connection to Anthropic API` from the command palette. On success the proxy log shows `[upstream] using proxy http://... for https upstream (rejectUnauthorized=true, ca=default)` on the first forwarded request.

claude-code-cache-fix.noProxy.markdownDescription:
Comma-separated host list to bypass the corporate proxy. Matches curl/Python/Go semantics.

- `*` — bypass all
- `.suffix.example` — bypass that suffix and any subdomain
- bare host — bypass exact match and `*.host`

The default covers loopback and `.local` mDNS. Add your corporate domain suffix (e.g. `.corp.example`) to bypass internal services.

claude-code-cache-fix.caFile.markdownDescription:
Path to a PEM file with extra CA certificates, for SSL-inspecting proxies (Zscaler, Netskope, corp MITM).

Leave empty to use the system-default trust store. Alternative to setting the system-wide `NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS` environment variable — setting it here scopes the CA to the cache-fix proxy only.

On Windows, enable `autoExportCerts` below to have the extension generate this bundle automatically from the Windows certificate store.

claude-code-cache-fix.rejectUnauthorized.markdownDescription:
**Verify TLS certificates on upstream forwarding.** Keep `true` unless you have no other option.

**Setting to `false` is an insecure escape hatch** — disables all cert validation. Use only as a temporary workaround while IT provides the correct corporate CA bundle; re-enable as soon as you can. When disabled, the proxy prints a one-time warning to the output channel on first request.

claude-code-cache-fix.autoExportCerts.markdownDescription:
**Windows only.** On activate, export matching certificates from `Cert:\LocalMachine\Root|CA` and `Cert:\CurrentUser\Root|CA` (subject-pattern matched — see `certSearchPatterns`) to `~/.claude/cache-fix-corp-ca-bundle.pem`. When `caFile` is empty, this path is automatically used.

Useful for Zscaler / Netskope / Forcepoint environments where the CA chain lives in the Windows trust store but Node doesn't read from there.

Idempotent — reruns pick up cert rotations.

claude-code-cache-fix.certSearchPatterns.markdownDescription:
Certificate subject patterns for `autoExportCerts` to match against. PowerShell `-like` wildcards, matched against each cert's Subject field.

Add your corporate CA's distinguishing substring (e.g. `*YourCorp*`) if it isn't one of the default MITM vendors. You can also add multiple if IT uses different CAs for different environments.

configuration.title: Claude Code Cache Fix: Pipeline extensions (opt-out toggles)

claude-code-cache-fix.skipFingerprint.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Strips the unstable `cc_version` fingerprint (e.g. `2.1.92.a3f`) from the system prompt before forwarding. Claude Code computes this fingerprint from `messages[0]` content including meta/attachment blocks — when blocks shift position on resume, the fingerprint changes, the system prompt changes, and the entire prefix cache busts.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `fingerprint-strip.enabled=false` in the proxy override.

**Cost of skipping:** In a validated 7.5-hour Windows session (536 calls), 81% of calls had fingerprint instability — each one a full cache miss (~6-30K tokens of `cache_creation` instead of `cache_read`). Cache hit rate drops from 95-99% to 60-80% on resume sessions.

Pipeline extension `fingerprint-strip`, order 100. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#1-fingerprint-strip-order-100).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipToolSort.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Sorts tool and MCP-server definitions alphabetically by name before forwarding. Tool definitions can otherwise arrive in different orders between turns; since the API cache key hashes the full request body, different tool ordering produces a different cache key and a full cache miss.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `sort-stabilization.enabled=false`.

**Cost of skipping:** On sessions with 3+ MCP servers, tool-order jitter was observed on ~15% of calls. Each jitter event triggers a full prefix rebuild: 150K-400K tokens of `cache_creation` depending on context size.

Pipeline extension `sort-stabilization`, order 200. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#2-sort-stabilization-order-200).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipSessionStartNo.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Applies the same block sorting to the first turn of a fresh session that Claude Code applies to subsequent turns. CC's `normalizeResumeMessages` has an early-return on `length < 2` that skips sorting — so the first call after `/clear` or a new session has unsorted blocks, busting the cache prefix for that turn.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `fresh-session-sort.enabled=false`.

**Cost of skipping:** First turn of every fresh session gets a full cache miss. Not catastrophic (one miss per session) but expensive on sessions with large system prompts.

Pipeline extension `fresh-session-sort`, order 250. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#3-fresh-session-sort-order-250).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipSmooshSplit.markdownDescription:
**Fix (v3.1.0 default-on):** Peels `system-reminder` blocks out of tool results so they don't smoosh against the actual tool output and destabilize the cache boundary.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `smoosh-split.enabled=false`.

**Cost of skipping:** System-reminder content mixed into tool results changes the request body hash per turn, partial cache misses accumulate. Real-world testing of this + `content-strip` + `tool-input-normalize` together recovered cache hit rate from 5.9% to 99.9% in ~2 calls.

Pipeline extension `smoosh-split`, order 320 (new in v3.1.0).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipContentStrip.markdownDescription:
**Fix (v3.1.0 default-on):** Strips per-turn bookkeeping text (todo-list nags, usage warnings, session-state reminders) that Claude Code injects into request bodies. These mutate per turn and destabilize the cache.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `content-strip.enabled=false`.

Pipeline extension `content-strip`, order 330 (new in v3.1.0).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipToolInputNorma.markdownDescription:
**Fix (v3.1.0 default-on):** Normalizes JSON serialization of `tool_use` input fields — key order, whitespace — so the same logical call doesn't produce different cache keys when re-issued.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `tool-input-normalize.enabled=false`.

Pipeline extension `tool-input-normalize`, order 340 (new in v3.1.0).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipCacheControlNo.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Pins `cache_control` markers (the `{"type": "ephemeral"}` annotations that tell the API what to cache) at canonical positions — the last block of the last user message. Without normalization, markers can drift between turns, shifting the cache boundary and causing partial cache misses.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `cache-control-normalize.enabled=false`.

**Cost of skipping:** Marker drift causes partial cache misses on each turn. Not full misses, but enough to increase `cache_creation` steadily over the session.

Pipeline extension `cache-control-normalize`, order 400. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#5-cache-control-normalize-order-400).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipTtl.markdownDescription:
**Fix:** Detects the server's cache TTL tier (1-hour or 5-minute) and injects appropriate `cache_control` ephemeral markers to maximize reuse within that window. On cold starts, the server assigns a 5-minute TTL until the first cached call promotes to 1-hour; TTL management accelerates that promotion.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `ttl-management.enabled=false`.

**Cost of skipping:** Requests may lack TTL markers entirely, leaving caching to server defaults which may not be optimal. An A/B test on CC v2.1.117 showed the proxy (all extensions on) achieved a 95.5% cache hit rate vs 82.3% direct on the first warm turn — TTL management is a significant contributor to that 13-point gap.

Pipeline extension `ttl-management`, order 500. See [impact guide](https://github.com/cnighswonger/claude-code-cache-fix/blob/main/docs/extension-impact-guide.md#6-ttl-management-order-500).

claude-code-cache-fix.skipDeferredToolsR.markdownDescription:
**Fix (v3.1.0 default-on, env-var controlled):** Persists clean `deferred_tools` blocks and restores them when an MCP server reconnects mid-session. Without this fix, MCP reconnect race conditions can shrink tool attachments and trigger massive cache misses.

**When this setting is `true`, the fix is skipped** — sets `CACHE_FIX_SKIP_DEFERRED_TOOLS_RESTORE=1` in the proxy spawn env.

**Cost of skipping:** Any session that uses MCP and survives a server reconnect (network blip, server restart, transient timeout) eats a full prefix rebuild on the next turn. New in v3.1.0; addresses a class of cache misses that wasn't covered by earlier extensions.
